steps.title: Solve the system for the intersection point
steps.content:
Start with the two equations:
$$x + 5y = 5$$
$$2x - y = -4$$
Solve the first for $x$: $x = 5 - 5y$.
Substitute into the second:
$$2(5 - 5y) - y = -4$$
Simplify:
$$10 - 10y - y = -4$$
$$10 - 11y = -4$$
$$-11y = -14$$
$$y = \frac{14}{11}$$
Then $x = 5 - 5\cdot\frac{14}{11} = 5 - \frac{70}{11} = \frac{55-70}{11} = -\frac{15}{11}$.
So the intersection point is $\left(-\frac{15}{11},\frac{14}{11}\right)$.

steps.title: Identify the matching graph
steps.content:
The intersection point is approximately (−1.36, 1.27).
Among the four graphs, only the credited option depicts two lines that cross at a point with those coordinates.
That confirms C is correct.
